title: Pleione dilamellata
intro: Pleione dilamellata, also known as the Himalayan Pleione, is a type of orchid species native to the Himalayan region. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleione dilamellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pleione dilamellata is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, often used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Pleione dilamellata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the orchid family. This species belongs to the genus Pleione, a group of terrestrial orchids native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The family Orchidaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Pleione dilamellata being part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is the largest and most diverse group within the orchid family, containing many species of orchids. Pleione dilamellata is specifically classified as a terrestrial orchid, which implies that it grows on land rather than in water or as an epiphyte.
